data.content: One file with one call site. I'll add `System.IO.Abstractions`, inject `IFileSystem`, and replace the static call.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-ef98b686d33d4754a85eab3bd42180cd/MyProject && dotnet add package System.IO.Abstractions --no-restore -q 2>&1 && dotnet restore -q 2>&1

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to replace `File.ReadAllText` with `IFileSystem`. This is a testability migration task. Let me use the `migrate-static-to-wrapper` skill since the user explicitly wants to do bulk replacement of static calls.

Actually, looking at the available skills, I have:
- `migrate-static-to-wrapper` - "Mechanically replace static dependency call sites with wrapper or built-in abstraction calls across a bounded scope"
- `generate-testability-wrappers` - "Generate wrapper interfaces and DI registration for hard-to-test static dependencies"

The user said "Replace all File.ReadAllText calls in my project with IFileSystem. Just do the bulk replacement across all files." This is a straightforward mechanical migration. Let me use the `migrate-static-to-wrapper` skill.
 
Actually, since this is just one file with a single call, I could handle it manually instead. But using the skill ensures I follow the proper patterns with System.IO.Abstractions and constructor injection, so let me just do it directly: I'll add the NuGet package, inject IFileSystem through the constructor, and swap out the static File call.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-ef98b686d33d4754a85eab3bd42180cd/MyProject && dotnet add package System.IO.Abstractions --no-restore -q 2>&1 && dotnet restore -q 2>&1

data.content:
Build succeeded. Here's what I did in `DataLoader.cs`:

1. Added `System.IO.Abstractions` NuGet package
2. Added constructor injection for `IFileSystem`
3. Replaced `File.ReadAllText(path)` → `_fileSystem.File.ReadAllText(path)`

In tests, you can inject `MockFileSystem` from the companion package `System.IO.Abstractions.TestingHelpers` to avoid touching the real file system.
